text: If \( A \) is a symmetric matrix, then \( A^T = \)
explanation: A matrix is symmetric if \( A^T = A \) (transpose equals itself).
name: Symmetric Matrix
subject: maths

text: The form factor of a sinusoidal AC wave is:
explanation: Form factor \( = V_{rms}/V_{avg} = \frac{V_0/\sqrt{2}}{2V_0/\pi} = \frac{\pi}{2\sqrt{2}} \approx 1.11 \). Peak factor \( = V_0/V_{rms} = \sqrt{2} \approx 1.414 \).
name: Form Factor
subject: phy

text: Phenol gives a violet/purple colour with FeCl₃ due to formation of:
explanation: Phenol + FeCl₃ → violet/purple iron(III) phenoxide complex. Used as a test for phenols. Enols also give positive test.
name: FeCl₃ Test for Phenol
subject: che

text: For ellipse \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \), eccentricity \( e = \)
explanation: Eccentricity of ellipse: \( e = \frac{c}{a} \) where \( c = \sqrt{a^2-b^2} \). Always \( 0 < e < 1 \).
name: Eccentricity of Ellipse
subject: maths

text: The coordination number of Co in [Co(en)₂Cl₂]⁺ (en = ethylenediamine) is:
explanation: en is a bidentate ligand (occupies 2 positions). 2 en × 2 = 4 positions + 2 Cl = 6. Coordination number = 6.
name: Coordination Number with Bidentate
subject: che

text: \( \int_0^{\pi/2}\sin^n x\,dx = \int_0^{\pi/2}\cos^n x\,dx \). This follows from:
explanation: Applying \( x \to \frac{\pi}{2}-x \): \( \sin x \to \cos x \), so both integrals are equal.
name: sin-cos Symmetry
subject: maths

text: \( |\vec{a}\times\vec{b}| = \)
explanation: \( |\vec{a}\times\vec{b}| = |\vec{a}||\vec{b}|\sin\theta \). Area of parallelogram = \( |\vec{a}\times\vec{b}| \).
name: Magnitude of Cross Product
subject: maths

text: The tangent to parabola \( y^2=4ax \) at point \((at^2, 2at)\) is:
explanation: Tangent at parametric point \((at^2,2at)\) on \(y^2=4ax\): \( ty = x + at^2 \)
name: Parametric Tangent to Parabola
subject: maths

text: The rotational analogue of Newton's second law is:
explanation: Torque = moment of inertia × angular acceleration: \( \vec{\tau} = I\vec{\alpha} \). Analogous to \( F = ma \).
name: Rotational Newton's 2nd Law
subject: phy

text: Hardy-Schulze rule states that coagulating power of an ion is:
explanation: Hardy-Schulze Rule: higher the valency of the coagulating ion, greater its coagulating power. Al³⁺ > Ca²⁺ > Na⁺ for negative sol.
name: Hardy-Schulze Rule
subject: che

text: Newton's second law states:
options: Every action has equal and opposite reaction
A body in rest stays at rest
explanation: Newton's Second Law: \( \vec{F}_{net} = m\vec{a} \). More precisely: \( \vec{F} = \frac{d\vec{p}}{dt} \) (rate of change of momentum).
name: Newton's Second Law
subject: phy

text: The function \( f(x) = |x| \) is:
explanation: \( f(-x) = |-x| = |x| = f(x) \). So \(f\) is an even function.
name: Even or Odd Function
subject: maths

text: The number of lone pairs on the central atom of \( \text{XeF}_4 \) is:
explanation: Xe in \(\text{XeF}_4\): 4 bond pairs + 2 lone pairs → \(sp^3d^2\) → square planar shape.
name: Lone Pairs in XeF₄
subject: che

text: Brewster's angle (polarising angle) \(\theta_B\) is related to refractive index by:
explanation: Brewster's law: \( \tan\theta_B = n \) (\(n\) = refractive index of second medium). At this angle, reflected light is completely plane-polarised.
name: Brewster's Law
subject: phy

text: Ozonolysis of alkenes followed by reductive workup (Zn/H₂O) gives:
explanation: Ozonolysis + reductive workup (Zn/H₂O): cleaves C=C bond → aldehydes (from =CH₂ or =CHR) and ketones (from =CR₂). Oxidative workup (H₂O₂) → carboxylic acids.
name: Ozonolysis Products
subject: che

text: The potential gradient (\(dV/dr\)) at the surface of a charged conductor is related to surface charge density \(\sigma\) by:
explanation: Just outside conductor: \( E = \sigma/\epsilon_0 \) and \( E = -dV/dr \Rightarrow dV/dr = -\sigma/\epsilon_0 \). (Negative: potential decreases away from +ve charge)
name: Potential Gradient at Conductor Surface
subject: phy

text: If \( f(x) = \frac{|x|}{x} \), then at \( x=0 \), \(f\) is:
explanation: \( \lim_{x\to 0^+}f(x)=1 \) and \( \lim_{x\to 0^-}f(x)=-1 \). LHL ≠ RHL, so discontinuous at \(x=0\).
name: Discontinuity Check
subject: maths

text: The radical axis of two circles is the locus of points from which tangents to both circles are:
explanation: The radical axis is the locus of points from which the tangent lengths to both circles are equal.
name: Radical Axis Definition
subject: maths

text: The maximum value of \( -x^2 + 4x - 3 \) is:
explanation: Vertex at \( x = -\frac{b}{2a} = 2 \). Max value \( = -(4) + 8 - 3 = 1 \)
name: Maximum of Quadratic
subject: maths

text: The order of reaction is determined by:
options: The stoichiometry of the balanced equation
explanation: Order must be determined experimentally. It may or may not match stoichiometric coefficients.
name: Determining Reaction Order
subject: che

text: \( \cos 2\theta = \)
explanation: \( \cos 2\theta = 2\cos^2\theta - 1 = 1 - 2\sin^2\theta = \cos^2\theta - \sin^2\theta \)
name: Double Angle - cos
subject: maths

text: DDT (dichlorodiphenyltrichloroethane) is:
explanation: DDT: chlorinated insecticide, non-biodegradable (persists in environment, accumulates in food chain). Banned in most countries now.
name: DDT
subject: che

text: The gravitational field intensity at a distance \(r\) outside a uniform sphere of mass \(M\) is:
explanation: Outside sphere: \( g = \frac{GM}{r^2} \) (same as point mass). Inside: \( g = \frac{GM}{R^3}r \propto r \).
name: Gravitational Field
subject: phy

text: For a zero-order reaction, the half-life is:
name: Zero Order Half-life
subject: che

text: If \( P(A) = 0.4 \) and \( P(B) = 0.5 \) and \( A, B \) are mutually exclusive, then \( P(A \cup B) = \)
explanation: Mutually exclusive: \( P(A \cup B) = P(A)+P(B) = 0.4+0.5 = 0.9 \)
name: Mutually Exclusive Events
subject: maths

text: The term independent of \(x\) in \( \left(x + \frac{1}{x}\right)^6 \) is:
name: Term Independent of x
subject: maths

text: A torque of 10 N·m acts on a body with moment of inertia 2 kg·m². Its angular acceleration is:
explanation: \( \alpha = \tau/I = 10/2 = 5\,\text{rad/s}^2 \). Analogous to \( a = F/m \).
name: Angular Acceleration Calculation
subject: phy

text: The equation of plane through three points can be found using:
explanation: Plane through 3 points: \( \begin{vmatrix}x-x_1&y-y_1&z-z_1\\x_2-x_1&y_2-y_1&z_2-z_1\\x_3-x_1&y_3-y_1&z_3-z_1\end{vmatrix}=0 \)
name: Plane Through 3 Points
subject: maths

text: Stefan's law of radiation states that radiated power per unit area is:
explanation: Stefan-Boltzmann law: \( P = \epsilon\sigma A T^4 \), where \(\sigma = 5.67\times10^{-8}\,\text{W/m}^2\text{K}^4\) and \(\epsilon\) = emissivity.
name: Stefan's Law
subject: phy

text: Electron affinity is the energy change when:
options: A neutral atom loses an electron
A neutral gaseous atom gains one electron
explanation: Electron affinity (EA): energy released when a neutral gaseous atom gains an electron to form anion. Cl has highest EA.
name: Electron Affinity
subject: che

text: Which molecule has zero dipole moment despite polar bonds?
explanation: \(\text{CO}_2\) is linear; two C=O dipoles cancel → net dipole = 0. \(\text{H}_2\text{O}\) and \(\text{NH}_3\) have net dipole.
name: Zero Dipole Moment
subject: che

text: The condition for \( lx+my+n=0 \) to be tangent to ellipse \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \) is:
explanation: Tangent condition to ellipse: \( a^2l^2+b^2m^2=n^2 \)
name: Tangent Condition to Ellipse
subject: maths

text: By Descartes' rule, the maximum number of positive roots of \( x^3 - 3x^2 + 4 = 0 \) is:
explanation: Sign changes: \(+, -, +\) — 2 sign changes, so max 2 positive roots (or 0).
name: Descartes' Rule of Signs
subject: maths

text: If \( \alpha^2 + \beta^2 = 5 \) and \( \alpha\beta = 2 \), then \( (\alpha - \beta)^2 = \)
explanation: \( (\alpha-\beta)^2 = \alpha^2+\beta^2 - 2\alpha\beta = 5 - 4 = 1 \)
name: Difference of Roots Squared
subject: maths

text: The angular frequency \(\omega\) of SHM with spring constant \(k\) and mass \(m\) is:
explanation: Spring-mass system: \( \omega = \sqrt{k/m} \), \( T = 2\pi\sqrt{m/k} \), \( f = \frac{1}{2\pi}\sqrt{k/m} \).
name: Angular Frequency of Spring-Mass
subject: phy

text: The law of conservation of mass states that in a chemical reaction:
options: Mass of products > mass of reactants
Mass of reactants = mass of products
Mass is always lost as energy
explanation: Lavoisier's Law: Total mass of reactants = Total mass of products. Matter is neither created nor destroyed.
name: Conservation of Mass
subject: che

text: Which of the following has the smallest size?
explanation: All are isoelectronic (10 electrons). Higher nuclear charge → smaller size. Al³⁺ (Z=13) smallest, Na (Z=11) largest among ions.
name: Smallest Isoelectronic Size
subject: che

text: The equilibrium constant \(K\) is related to \(E^\circ\) by:
explanation: At 25°C: \( \log K = \frac{nE^\circ}{0.0592} \) (from \( \Delta G^\circ = -nFE^\circ = -RT\ln K \)).
name: K from E°
subject: che

text: The scalar triple product \( [\vec{a}\,\vec{b}\,\vec{c}] = \vec{a}\cdot(\vec{b}\times\vec{c}) \) gives:
explanation: \( |[\vec{a}\,\vec{b}\,\vec{c}]| \) = volume of parallelepiped formed by \(\vec{a}, \vec{b}, \vec{c}\).
name: Scalar Triple Product
subject: maths

text: Relative velocity of A with respect to B is:
explanation: Relative velocity: \( \vec{v}_{A/B} = \vec{v}_A - \vec{v}_B \). For two trains moving in same direction at \(v_1, v_2\): relative speed \(= v_1 - v_2\).
name: Relative Velocity
subject: phy

text: The angle bisectors of \( ax^2+2hxy+by^2=0 \) are given by:
explanation: Equation of angle bisectors: \( \frac{x^2-y^2}{a-b} = \frac{xy}{h} \)
name: Angle Bisectors of Pair
subject: maths

text: The mean and variance of a data set are 5 and 4. The coefficient of variation is:
name: CV Calculation
subject: maths

text: Lanthanide contraction refers to:
options: Decrease in atomic radius across f-block due to poor shielding by f electrons
Increase in atomic radius in lanthanides
explanation: Lanthanide contraction: steady decrease in atomic/ionic radius across lanthanides due to poor shielding of 4f electrons. Causes 5d and 4d elements to have similar sizes.
name: Lanthanide Contraction
subject: che

text: A function is said to have a removable discontinuity at \(x=a\) if:
options: \( f(a) \) is undefined but limit exists
explanation: Removable discontinuity: \( \lim_{x\to a}f(x) \) exists but either \(f(a)\) is undefined or \( f(a)\neq\lim f(x) \).
name: Removable Discontinuity
subject: maths

text: For the Haber process \( \text{N}_2 + 3\text{H}_2 \rightleftharpoons 2\text{NH}_3 \), increasing pressure:
options: Shifts equilibrium to left (more N₂ and H₂)
explanation: Reactants: 4 mol gas; Products: 2 mol gas. Increasing pressure → shift to side with fewer moles of gas → right (more NH₃).
name: Haber Process and Pressure
subject: che

text: The α-helix and β-pleated sheet are examples of:
explanation: Secondary structure: regular folding maintained by H-bonds between C=O and N–H of peptide bonds. α-helix (coiled) and β-sheet (pleated).
name: Protein Secondary Structure
subject: che

text: The first law of thermodynamics is expressed as:
explanation: First Law: \( \Delta U = q - w \) (IUPAC convention: \(q\) = heat absorbed, \(w\) = work done BY system). Some texts use \( \Delta U = q + w \) (w = work done ON system).
name: First Law of Thermodynamics
subject: che

text: If one root of \( x^2 - 5x + k = 0 \) is 2, then \( k = \)
explanation: Substituting \( x=2 \): \( 4 - 10 + k = 0 \Rightarrow k = 6 \)
name: Finding k from Root
subject: maths

text: If the side of a square increases at 2 cm/s, the rate of increase of area when side = 5 cm is:
explanation: \( A=x^2 \Rightarrow \frac{dA}{dt} = 2x\frac{dx}{dt} = 2(5)(2) = 20 \text{ cm}^2/\text{s} \)
name: Related Rates
subject: maths

text: The Compton effect involves:
options: Scattering of X-ray photon by free electron (wavelength shift: \( \Delta\lambda = \frac{h}{m_ec}(1-\cos\phi) \))
explanation: Compton scattering: X-ray photon scattered by free electron. Wavelength increases: \( \Delta\lambda = \frac{h}{m_ec}(1-\cos\phi) \). Compton wavelength \( = h/m_ec = 0.0243\) Å.
name: Compton Effect
subject: phy

text: \( \lim_{x \to 0} \frac{\tan x}{x} = \)
explanation: \( \lim_{x\to 0}\frac{\tan x}{x} = \lim_{x\to 0}\frac{\sin x}{x}\cdot\frac{1}{\cos x} = 1\cdot1 = 1 \)
name: tan x / x Limit
subject: maths

text: The middle term in the expansion of \( (x+y)^8 \) is the:
explanation: For even \(n\), middle term is \( T_{\frac{n}{2}+1} = T_5 \) (5th term).
name: Middle Term
subject: maths

text: For proving \( P(n): 2^n > n \) for all \( n \geq 1 \), the base case is verified at \( n = \)
explanation: At \( n=1 \): \( 2^1 = 2 > 1 \). Base case holds.
name: Base Case Verification
subject: maths

text: The angular magnification of an astronomical telescope is:
explanation: Astronomical telescope: \( M = f_o/f_e \). Large \(f_o\) and small \(f_e\) → high magnification. Length \( = f_o + f_e \) (image at infinity).
name: Telescope Magnification
subject: phy

text: The inradius \(r\) of a triangle is:
explanation: Inradius \( r = \frac{\Delta}{s} \) where \( s = \frac{a+b+c}{2} \) is the semi-perimeter.
name: Inradius Formula
subject: maths

text: For an ideal gas, the adiabatic process follows:
explanation: Adiabatic process: \( PV^\gamma = \text{const} \), where \( \gamma = C_p/C_v > 1 \). More steep than isothermal curve on P-V diagram.
name: Adiabatic Process Equation
subject: phy

text: The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus: \( \int_a^b f(x)\,dx = \)
explanation: FTC: \( \int_a^b f(x)dx = F(b)-F(a) \), where \(F'(x)=f(x)\).
name: Fundamental Theorem
subject: maths

text: In an isothermal process for an ideal gas, work done is:
explanation: Isothermal work: \( W = nRT\ln\frac{V_2}{V_1} = nRT\ln\frac{P_1}{P_2} \). Temperature is constant → \( \Delta U = 0 \Rightarrow Q = W \).
name: Isothermal Work
subject: phy

text: A catalyst in a chemical equilibrium:
options: Increases rate of both forward and reverse reactions equally
Only increases rate of forward reaction
explanation: A catalyst increases rates of both forward and reverse reactions equally. It does not change K or shift equilibrium position.
name: Catalyst and Equilibrium
subject: che

text: Gauss's law states that the electric flux through a closed surface is:
explanation: Gauss's law: \( \Phi_E = \frac{Q_{enc}}{\epsilon_0} \). Flux depends only on enclosed charge, not on shape of surface.
name: Gauss's Law
subject: phy

explanation: Sum of first \(n\) odd numbers \( = n^2 \). Verify by induction.
name: Sum of Odd Numbers
subject: maths

text: The variation of \(g\) with depth \(d\) inside Earth is:
explanation: Inside Earth: \( g' = g\left(1 - \frac{d}{R}\right) \). At centre (d=R): g'=0. Decreases linearly going inside.
name: g Inside Earth
subject: phy

text: The resolving power of a telescope is proportional to:
options: Diameter of objective lens/mirror (larger aperture → better resolution)
explanation: Resolving power: \( \theta_{min} = 1.22\lambda/D \). Larger objective diameter \(D\) → smaller \(\theta_{min}\) → better resolution. Rayleigh criterion.
name: Resolving Power of Telescope
subject: phy

text: The energy of a photon with frequency \(\nu\) is:
explanation: Planck's equation: \( E = h\nu = \frac{hc}{\lambda} \), where \( h = 6.626 \times 10^{-34} \) J·s.
name: Planck's Equation
subject: che

text: Chloroform (CHCl₃) when oxidised in air slowly gives:
explanation: CHCl₃ oxidises in air to form phosgene (COCl₂), which is highly toxic. That's why CHCl₃ is stored in dark bottles with a little ethanol.
name: Oxidation of Chloroform
subject: che

text: Reduction of a nitrile (R–CN) with LiAlH₄ gives:
explanation: R–CN + LiAlH₄ → R–CH₂–NH₂ (primary amine). The nitrile carbon is reduced to –CH₂–. Useful synthetic route.
name: Reduction of Nitrile
subject: che

text: If \( \omega \) is a complex cube root of unity, then \( \omega^{100} + \omega^{200} + \omega^{300} = \)
explanation: \( 100 = 3\times33+1,\ 200=3\times66+2,\ 300=3\times100 \). So \( \omega^1 + \omega^2 + 1 = 0+1 \)... Correction: \( \omega^{300} = (\omega^3)^{100} = 1 \), \( \omega^{100}=\omega \), \( \omega^{200}=\omega^2 \). Sum \( = 1+\omega+\omega^2+1-1 = 3\cdot1 \) for this pattern gives 3.
name: Omega Powers Sum
subject: maths

text: \( \frac{1}{x(x+1)} = \frac{A}{x} + \frac{B}{x+1} \). What are \(A\) and \(B\)?
explanation: At \(x=0\): \(1 = A(1) \Rightarrow A=1\). At \(x=-1\): \(1 = B(-1) \Rightarrow B=-1\).
name: Basic Partial Fraction
subject: maths

text: In a stationary wave, nodes are points where:
explanation: Nodes: points of zero amplitude in standing waves. Antinodes: maximum amplitude. Distance between adjacent nodes = \(\lambda/2\).
name: Nodes in Standing Waves
subject: phy

text: An ideal solution obeys Raoult's law over the entire concentration range and has:
explanation: Ideal solution: \( \Delta H_{mix} = 0 \), \( \Delta V_{mix} = 0 \). Example: benzene + toluene.
name: Ideal Solution Properties
subject: che

text: The standard equation of an ellipse with semi-major axis \(a\) along x-axis is:
explanation: Ellipse: \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \) with \(a>b>0\). Foci at \( (\pm c,0) \) where \( c^2=a^2-b^2 \).
name: Ellipse Standard Form
subject: maths

text: \( \sin 3\theta = \)
explanation: Triple angle formula: \( \sin 3\theta = 3\sin\theta - 4\sin^3\theta \)
name: Triple Angle - sin
subject: maths

text: For resistors in series, the equivalent resistance is:
explanation: Series: \( R_{eq} = R_1 + R_2 + \cdots \). Current is same through all. Parallel: \( 1/R_{eq} = 1/R_1 + 1/R_2 \).
name: Resistors in Series
subject: phy

text: Amines react with acyl chloride (RCOCl) to give:
explanation: R'NH₂ + RCOCl → RCONHR' + HCl. Nucleophilic acyl substitution. Acylation of amine (Schotten-Baumann conditions).
name: Acylation of Amines
subject: che

text: Equipotential surfaces are always:
explanation: Equipotential surfaces: \( V = \text{const} \). Always perpendicular to \(\vec{E}\). For point charge: concentric spheres. For uniform field: parallel planes.
name: Equipotential Surfaces
subject: phy

text: The diameter of the \(n\)th dark ring in Newton's rings is:
explanation: Newton's rings dark ring: \( D_n = 2\sqrt{n\lambda R} \), where \(R\) = radius of curvature of lens. \(D_n^2 \propto n \) (linear with \(n\)).
name: Newton's Rings Diameter
subject: phy

text: The general solution of \( \cos\theta = 0 \) is:
explanation: \( \cos\theta = 0 \Rightarrow \theta = (2n+1)\frac{\pi}{2} \), where \( n \in \mathbb{Z} \).
name: General Solution of cosθ=0
subject: maths

text: The equation of tangent to \( y=f(x) \) at \( (x_1,y_1) \) is:
name: Equation of Tangent
subject: maths

text: Two lines with slopes \( m_1 \) and \( m_2 \) are perpendicular if:
explanation: Lines are perpendicular when \( m_1 \cdot m_2 = -1 \).
name: Perpendicular Lines
subject: maths

text: The rank of a \( 3 \times 3 \) identity matrix \( I_3 \) is:
explanation: The identity matrix is full rank. For \( I_3 \): rank \( = 3 \) since \( |I_3| = 1 \neq 0 \).
name: Rank of Identity Matrix
subject: maths

text: Optical isomerism (enantiomers) is possible in:
options: Octahedral complexes with three bidentate ligands [M(AA)₃]
explanation: Optical isomers (non-superimposable mirror images) occur in [M(AA)₃] type octahedral complexes (e.g., [Co(en)₃]³⁺). Also [M(AA)₂BC]²⁺.
name: Optical Isomerism in Complexes
subject: che

text: If \( f(x) = x^2 + 1 \) and \( g(x) = \sqrt{x} \), then \( f(g(x)) = \)
name: Composite Function
subject: maths

text: \( ^nC_r = \binom{n}{r} \) satisfies \( ^nC_r = \)
explanation: Symmetry property: \( \binom{n}{r} = \binom{n}{n-r} \)
name: Symmetry of Combinations
subject: maths

text: The inverse of \( f(x) = 2x + 3 \) is:
explanation: Set \( y = 2x+3 \Rightarrow x = \frac{y-3}{2} \). So \( f^{-1}(x) = \frac{x-3}{2} \)
name: Inverse Function
subject: maths

text: Number of 3-digit numbers formed from digits 1,2,3,4,5 (no repetition) is:
name: Digits without Repetition
subject: maths

text: The limiting reagent in a reaction is the one that:
options: Is completely consumed first and limits the product
explanation: The limiting reagent is completely consumed first and determines the maximum amount of product formed.
name: Limiting Reagent
subject: che

text: The impedance of a series LCR circuit is:
explanation: LCR impedance: \( Z = \sqrt{R^2+(X_L-X_C)^2} \), where \( X_L=\omega L \) and \( X_C=1/\omega C \).
name: LCR Impedance
subject: phy

text: Two coins are tossed. \( P(\text{exactly one head}) = \)
explanation: Sample space: \{HH, HT, TH, TT\}. Exactly one head: \{HT, TH\}. \( P = \frac{2}{4} = \frac{1}{2} \)
name: Coin Toss Probability
subject: maths

text: Total internal reflection occurs when:
options: Angle of incidence > critical angle AND light travels from denser to rarer medium
Light travels from rarer to denser medium
explanation: TIR conditions: (1) light goes from denser to rarer medium, (2) angle of incidence > critical angle \(\theta_c = \sin^{-1}(n_2/n_1)\). Used in optical fibres.
name: Total Internal Reflection
subject: phy

text: The general term (\( T_{r+1} \)) in the expansion of \( (x+y)^n \) is:
explanation: By Binomial Theorem: \( T_{r+1} = \binom{n}{r} x^{n-r} y^r \)
name: General Term Formula
subject: maths

text: Dalton's law of partial pressures states:
explanation: Dalton's Law: \( P_{total} = P_1 + P_2 + \cdots + P_n \). Partial pressure \( P_i = x_i \cdot P_{total} \).
name: Dalton's Law
subject: che

text: Ampere's circuital law states:
explanation: Ampere's law: \( \oint \vec{B}\cdot d\vec{l} = \mu_0 I_{enc} \). Used to find \(B\) for symmetric current distributions (solenoid, toroid).
name: Ampere's Circuital Law
subject: phy

text: The kinetic energy of rolling without slipping is:
explanation: Rolling KE = translational + rotational: \( KE = \frac{1}{2}mv^2 + \frac{1}{2}I\omega^2 \). For solid sphere: \( KE = \frac{7}{10}mv^2 \).
name: Rolling Kinetic Energy
subject: phy

text: For parametric equations \( x=f(t), y=g(t) \), \( \frac{dy}{dx} = \)
explanation: Parametric differentiation: \( \frac{dy}{dx} = \frac{dy/dt}{dx/dt} = \frac{g'(t)}{f'(t)} \)
name: Parametric Differentiation
subject: maths

text: The DE \( \frac{dy}{dx}+y\tan x = \sec x \) has integrating factor:
explanation: IF \( = e^{\int\tan x\,dx} = e^{\ln|\sec x|} = \sec x \)
name: IF Calculation
subject: maths

text: The equation of x-axis is:
explanation: The x-axis is the line where \( y = 0 \) for all values of \(x\).
name: Equation of Axes
subject: maths

text: The degree of polymerisation (DP) is defined as:
options: Number of monomer units in a polymer chain
explanation: Degree of polymerisation: DP = Molar mass of polymer / Molar mass of monomer. High DP → high molecular weight polymer.
name: Degree of Polymerisation
subject: che

text: The magnetic susceptibility \(\chi_m\) of a diamagnetic material is:
explanation: Diamagnetic: \( \chi_m < 0 \) (small negative). Paramagnetic: \( \chi_m > 0 \) (small positive). Ferromagnetic: \( \chi_m \gg 1 \) (large positive).
name: Diamagnetic Susceptibility
subject: phy

text: The self-inductance \(L\) of a coil is defined by:
explanation: Self-inductance: \( \varepsilon = -L\frac{dI}{dt} \). Also \( L = N\Phi_B/I \). Unit: Henry (H) = Vs/A. Opposes change in current.
name: Self-Inductance Definition
subject: phy

text: A point of inflection occurs where:
explanation: Inflection point: \( f''(x) = 0 \) AND \( f''\) changes sign there (concavity changes).
name: Point of Inflection
subject: maths

text: A spring of spring constant \(k\) compressed by \(x\) stores elastic PE equal to:
explanation: Elastic PE in spring: \( U = \frac{1}{2}kx^2 \). Work done against spring force \( = \frac{1}{2}kx^2 \).
name: Spring Potential Energy
subject: phy

text: A body moves with uniform speed in a circle. Its acceleration is:
explanation: Uniform circular motion: speed is constant but direction changes. Centripetal acceleration \( a = \frac{v^2}{r} \) directed towards centre.
name: Acceleration in Circular Motion
subject: phy

text: The equation of the circle passing through origin with center \((3,4)\) is:
explanation: \( r = \sqrt{9+16}=5 \). Circle: \((x-3)^2+(y-4)^2=25\) expands to \(x^2+y^2-6x-8y=0\). Both A and D are correct, but A is the general form.
name: Circle Through Origin
subject: maths

text: The DE \( \frac{dy}{dx} = \frac{y}{x} \) has solution:
explanation: \( \frac{dy}{y}=\frac{dx}{x} \Rightarrow \ln|y|=\ln|x|+c \Rightarrow y=Cx \)
name: Simple Separable DE
subject: maths

text: The circumradius \(R\) of a triangle is:
explanation: \( R = \frac{a}{2\sin A} = \frac{abc}{4\Delta} \), where \(\Delta\) is area of triangle.
name: Circumradius Formula
subject: maths

text: K₂Cr₂O₇ in acidic medium: the half reaction at cathode involves Cr going from:
explanation: \( \text{Cr}_2\text{O}_7^{2-} + 14\text{H}^+ + 6e^- \to 2\text{Cr}^{3+} + 7\text{H}_2\text{O} \). Cr: +6 → +3 (reduction).
name: K₂Cr₂O₇ Reduction
subject: che

text: In \( \text{SF}_6 \), the hybridisation of S is:
explanation: \(\text{SF}_6\): 6 bond pairs, 0 lone pairs → \(sp^3d^2\) hybridisation → octahedral shape.
name: Hybridisation in SF₆
subject: che

text: The mean of squared deviations from mean is called:
explanation: Variance = mean of squared deviations from mean. SD is its square root.
name: Variance Definition
subject: maths

text: The inverse of \( A = \begin{pmatrix} 2 & 1 \\ 5 & 3 \end{pmatrix} \) is:
explanation: \( |A| = 6-5=1 \). \( A^{-1} = \frac{1}{|A|}\begin{pmatrix}d & -b \\ -c & a\end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix}3 & -1 \\ -5 & 2\end{pmatrix} \)
name: 2x2 Inverse
subject: maths

text: Boron is a metalloid. Its hydride (diborane B₂H₆) has:
explanation: B₂H₆ has 2 three-centred two-electron (3c-2e) banana bonds (bridge H). Electron deficient compound.
name: Diborane Structure
subject: che

text: Raoult's law states that the vapour pressure of a solution is:
explanation: Raoult's Law: \( P = x_A P^\circ_A + x_B P^\circ_B \). For non-volatile solute: \( P = x_A P^\circ_A \).
name: Raoult's Law
subject: che

text: The number of real roots of \( x^4 + x^2 + 1 = 0 \) is:
explanation: \( x^4 + x^2 + 1 = (x^2 + \frac{1}{2})^2 + \frac{3}{4} > 0 \) for all real \(x\). No real roots.
name: No Real Roots Quartic
subject: maths

text: If the sum of two roots of \( x^3 + px^2 + qx + r = 0 \) is zero, then:
explanation: Let roots be \( \alpha, -\alpha, \beta \). Then \( \alpha+(-\alpha)+\beta = -p \Rightarrow \beta=-p \). Since \(\beta\) is root: use Vieta's relations to get \(pq = r\).
name: Condition on Roots Sum
subject: maths

text: In triangle \(ABC\), \( A + B + C = \)
explanation: Sum of angles in any triangle \( = 180° = \pi \) radians.
name: Angle Sum Property
subject: maths

text: \( \int e^x\,dx = \)
explanation: \( \int e^x\,dx = e^x+C \)
name: Integral of eˣ
subject: maths

text: Partial fractions are primarily used for:
explanation: Partial fractions decompose rational functions into simpler fractions, making integration straightforward.
name: Application of Partial Fractions
subject: maths

text: Pascal's law states that pressure applied to an enclosed fluid:
options: Acts only at the point of application
Is transmitted equally in all directions throughout the fluid
explanation: Pascal's Law: pressure applied to a confined fluid is transmitted undiminished in all directions (hydraulic press principle).
name: Pascal's Law
subject: phy

text: \( 1 + \tan^2\theta = \)
explanation: Dividing \( \sin^2+\cos^2=1 \) by \(\cos^2\): \( \tan^2+1 = \sec^2\theta \)
name: Pythagorean Identity 2
subject: maths

text: If \( P(A)=\frac{1}{2}, P(B)=\frac{1}{3} \) and \(A, B\) are independent, then \( P(A \cap B) = \)
explanation: \( P(A \cap B) = P(A) \cdot P(B) = \frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{3} = \frac{1}{6} \)
name: Independent Events Intersection
subject: maths

text: Kirchhoff's voltage law (KVL) states:
options: Current is constant around a loop
Sum of all voltages (EMFs and drops) around any closed loop = 0
Voltage is same across all elements
explanation: KVL (loop rule): \( \sum V = \sum EMF - \sum IR = 0 \). Based on conservation of energy.
name: Kirchhoff's Voltage Law
subject: phy

text: In a medium with refractive index \(n\), the speed of EM wave is:
explanation: Speed in medium: \( v = c/n \), where \( n = \sqrt{\mu_r\epsilon_r} \). Light slows down in denser media (higher \(n\)).
name: Speed of EM Wave in Medium
subject: phy

text: The principal value range of \( \sin^{-1}x \) is:
explanation: Domain of \( \sin^{-1}x \) is \([-1,1]\) and range (principal branch) is \(\left[-\frac{\pi}{2}, \frac{\pi}{2}\right]\).
name: Range of sin⁻¹
subject: maths

text: The AM-GM inequality states: for positive reals \(a\) and \(b\):
explanation: AM ≥ GM: \( \frac{a+b}{2} \geq \sqrt{ab} \). Equality holds iff \(a = b\).
name: AM-GM Inequality
subject: maths

text: \( \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}\,dx = \)
explanation: Standard result: \( \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}\,dx = \sin^{-1}x+C \)
name: Arcsin Integral
subject: maths

text: The oxoacids of phosphorus: phosphoric acid (H₃PO₄) has how many P–OH groups (proton donating)?
explanation: H₃PO₄ (orthophosphoric acid): 3 P–OH groups → tribasic acid. H₃PO₃: 2 P–OH → dibasic. H₃PO₂: 1 P–OH → monobasic.
name: Basicity of H₃PO₄
subject: che

text: The electric field \(\vec{E}\) at a point is defined as:
options: Force on any charge placed there
Force per unit positive test charge: \( \vec{E} = \vec{F}/q_0 \)
explanation: Electric field: \( \vec{E} = \vec{F}/q_0 \) (force per unit positive test charge). Units: N/C = V/m.
name: Definition of Electric Field
subject: phy

explanation: \( \sin^{-1} \) is an odd function: \( \sin^{-1}(-x) = -\sin^{-1}(x) \).
name: Odd Function Property
subject: maths

text: The magnetic flux \(\Phi_B\) through a loop of area \(A\) in field \(B\) at angle \(\theta\) is:
explanation: Magnetic flux: \( \Phi_B = \vec{B}\cdot\vec{A} = BA\cos\theta \), where \(\theta\) is angle between \(\vec{B}\) and area normal \(\hat{n}\). Unit: Weber (Wb).
name: Magnetic Flux Formula
subject: phy

text: The rectangular hyperbola has eccentricity:
explanation: Rectangular hyperbola: \( a=b \Rightarrow c=\sqrt{a^2+a^2}=a\sqrt{2} \Rightarrow e = c/a = \sqrt{2} \).
name: Rectangular Hyperbola
subject: maths

text: If \( f(x) \) is an even function, then \( \int_{-a}^{a} f(x)\,dx = \)
explanation: Even function property: \( \int_{-a}^{a}f(x)dx = 2\int_0^a f(x)dx \)
name: Even Function Integral
subject: maths

text: Benzaldehyde (PhCHO) is distinguished from acetaldehyde (CH₃CHO) by:
options: Fehling's test (PhCHO does not reduce Fehling's, CH₃CHO does)
explanation: Fehling's test: CH₃CHO → brick-red Cu₂O (positive). PhCHO → no reaction (negative). Both give positive Tollens' test.
name: Distinguishing Aldehydes
subject: che

text: The number of orbitals in a subshell with \(l=2\) (d subshell) is:
explanation: Number of orbitals in a subshell \( = 2l+1 \). For \(l=2\): \(2(2)+1=5\) orbitals.
name: Orbitals in d Subshell
subject: che

text: A fair die is rolled. The probability of getting a number \( > 4 \) is:
name: Simple Probability - Die
subject: maths

text: If \( \sin\theta = \cos\theta \), then the general solution is:
explanation: \( \sin\theta = \cos\theta \Rightarrow \tan\theta = 1 \Rightarrow \theta = n\pi + \frac{\pi}{4} \)
name: sinθ = cosθ
subject: maths

text: Acetic acid (CH₃COOH) reacts with NH₃ followed by heating to give:
explanation: CH₃COOH + NH₃ → CH₃COONH₄ (ammonium salt) → heat → CH₃CONH₂ (acetamide) + H₂O.
name: Acid to Amide
subject: che

text: The velocity in SHM as a function of displacement is:
explanation: \( v^2 = \omega^2(A^2-x^2) \Rightarrow v = \omega\sqrt{A^2-x^2} \). At \(x=0\): \(v=A\omega\). At \(x=A\): \(v=0\).
name: Velocity-Displacement Relation in SHM
subject: phy

text: The packing efficiency of FCC (cubic close packing) is:
explanation: Packing efficiency: SC = 52.4%, BCC = 68%, FCC/HCP = 74% (maximum possible for equal spheres).
name: FCC Packing Efficiency
subject: che

text: The value of \( i^{97} \) is:
explanation: \( i^{97} = i^{4 \times 24 + 1} = i^1 = i \). Powers of \(i\) cycle with period 4.
name: Powers of i
subject: maths

text: In a perfectly inelastic collision:
options: Objects stick together and momentum is conserved
Both KE and momentum are conserved
explanation: Perfectly inelastic: maximum KE loss, objects stick together. Momentum IS conserved (always). KE is NOT conserved.
name: Perfectly Inelastic Collision
subject: phy

text: The isotopes of hydrogen are:
explanation: H isotopes: Protium (¹H, no neutron), Deuterium (²H or D, 1 neutron), Tritium (³H or T, 2 neutrons). Tritium is radioactive.
name: Hydrogen Isotopes
subject: che

text: Two lines \(a_1x+b_1y+c_1=0\) and \(a_2x+b_2y+c_2=0\) are parallel if:
explanation: Parallel condition: \( \frac{a_1}{a_2} = \frac{b_1}{b_2} \neq \frac{c_1}{c_2} \), i.e., \( a_1b_2 - a_2b_1 = 0 \).
name: Parallel Lines Condition
subject: maths

text: One Faraday equals:
explanation: 1 Faraday = 96500 C/mol = charge of 1 mole of electrons = \( N_A \times e \).
name: Faraday Constant
subject: che

text: For a compound to be aromatic by Hückel's rule, it must have:
options: (4n+2) π electrons in a planar cyclic conjugated system
An even number of π electrons
explanation: Hückel's rule: aromatic if planar, cyclic, fully conjugated, with (4n+2) π electrons (n = 0,1,2...). Benzene: n=1, 6π electrons.
name: Hückel's Rule
subject: che

text: The Doppler effect formula for source moving towards stationary observer is:
explanation: Source approaching observer: \( f' = f\frac{v}{v - v_s} > f \) (higher pitch). Source receding: \( f' = f\frac{v}{v + v_s} < f \).
name: Doppler Effect - Source Moving
subject: phy

text: In E2 elimination, the leaving group and the β-hydrogen must be:
explanation: E2 (bimolecular elimination): concerted, requires anti-periplanar arrangement (180° dihedral) of H and leaving group for effective overlap.
name: E2 Stereochemistry
subject: che

text: The length of tangent from external point \((x_1,y_1)\) to circle \( x^2+y^2+2gx+2fy+c=0 \) is:
explanation: Length of tangent \( = \sqrt{S_1} = \sqrt{x_1^2+y_1^2+2gx_1+2fy_1+c} \)
name: Length of Tangent
subject: maths

text: The solution of \( \cos\theta = -\frac{1}{2} \) in \( [0, 2\pi] \) is:
explanation: \( \cos\theta = -\frac{1}{2} \) in 2nd and 3rd quadrant: \( \theta = \frac{2\pi}{3} \) and \( \frac{4\pi}{3} \).
name: cos = -1/2 Solutions
subject: maths

text: \( \int \frac{1}{x}\,dx = \)
explanation: \( \int \frac{1}{x}\,dx = \ln|x|+C \). Special case of power rule when \(n=-1\).
name: Integral of 1/x
subject: maths

text: Bulk modulus is defined as:
explanation: Bulk modulus: \( B = -\frac{\Delta P}{\Delta V/V} \). Negative sign because pressure increase causes volume decrease. Liquids/gases have only bulk modulus.
name: Bulk Modulus
subject: phy

text: \( \sinh(x+y) = \)
explanation: \( \sinh(x+y) = \sinh x \cosh y + \cosh x \sinh y \) (analogous to \(\sin(A+B)\))
name: sinh(x+y) Formula
subject: maths

text: The vector equation of line through point \(\vec{a}\) in direction \(\vec{b}\) is:
explanation: Vector equation of line: \( \vec{r} = \vec{a} + \lambda\vec{b} \), where \(\lambda \in \mathbb{R}\).
name: Vector Equation of Line
subject: maths

text: \( \frac{d}{dx}(a^x) = \)
explanation: \( \frac{d}{dx}(a^x) = a^x\ln a \). When \(a=e\): reduces to \(e^x\).
name: Derivative of aˣ
subject: maths

text: The value of \( ^{10}P_3 \) is:
explanation: \( ^nP_r = \frac{n!}{(n-r)!} \Rightarrow ^{10}P_3 = \frac{10!}{7!} = 10 \times 9 \times 8 = 720 \)
name: Permutation Formula
subject: maths

text: The sky wave propagation uses which layer of atmosphere:
options: Ionosphere (reflects radio waves back to Earth)
explanation: Sky wave propagation: radio waves (2-30 MHz) reflected by ionosphere → can reach beyond horizon. Used for short-wave (HF) radio broadcasting.
name: Sky Wave Propagation
subject: phy

text: The pressure of an ideal gas is given by kinetic theory as:
explanation: Kinetic theory pressure: \( P = \frac{1}{3}\rho\overline{v^2} = \frac{1}{3}\rho v_{rms}^2 \). This derivation assumes elastic collisions and random motion.
name: Kinetic Theory Pressure Formula
subject: phy

text: A square matrix \( A \) is singular if:
explanation: A matrix is singular if and only if its determinant is zero.
name: Singular Matrix
subject: maths

text: \( \cot^2\theta - \csc^2\theta = \)
explanation: From \( 1 + \cot^2\theta = \csc^2\theta \): \( \cot^2\theta - \csc^2\theta = -1 \)
name: Pythagorean Identity 3
subject: maths

text: \( \frac{d}{dx}\int_a^{g(x)}f(t)\,dt = \)
explanation: Leibniz rule: \( \frac{d}{dx}\int_a^{g(x)}f(t)dt = f(g(x))\cdot g'(x) \) (chain rule applied).
name: Leibniz Rule
subject: maths

text: \( \frac{d}{dx}(x^n) = \)
explanation: Power Rule: \( \frac{d}{dx}(x^n) = nx^{n-1} \)
name: Power Rule
subject: maths
